title: Integrated sensor assembly
abstract: An integrated sensor assembly is described. The integrated sensor assembly can include a housing comprising a compartment. The housing can be arranged on a side surface of a cabin of an autonomous vehicle and within a threshold distance of a cabin roof of the autonomous vehicle. The assembly can include a first sensor arranged in the compartment, the first sensor configured to receive a first signal from a vehicle computer and collect information for mapping an environment of the autonomous vehicle. The assembly can include a light source arranged in the compartment. The light source configured to receive a second signal from the vehicle computer and emit a light pattern for visually indicating one of a hazard, a turn, or an autonomous mode. The light source can be arranged in the compartment to emit light to traffic toward a rear of the autonomous vehicle.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An integrated sensor assembly, comprising: a housing comprising a compartment, the housing arranged on a side surface of a cabin of an autonomous vehicle and a threshold elevation from a reference surface; a first sensor configured to receive a first signal from a vehicle computer and collect information for mapping an environment of the autonomous vehicle; and a light source arranged in the compartment, the light source configured to receive a second signal from the vehicle computer and emit a light pattern for visually indicating one of a hazard, a turn, or an autonomous mode, the light source arranged in the compartment to emit light to traffic toward a rear of the autonomous vehicle. 2. The integrated sensor assembly of claim 1, wherein the integrated sensor assembly further comprises: an arm connected at a first end to the housing and connected at a second end to the side surface of the cabin, the arm arranged orthogonally from the side surface of the cabin. 3. The integrated sensor assembly of any of claims 1 or 2, wherein the arm is extendable in an orthogonal direction from the side surface of the cabin. 4. The integrated sensor assembly of any of claims 1-3, wherein the housing and the arm are integrally formed. 5. The integrated sensor assembly of any of claims 1-4, wherein the light source comprises a first bulb for visually indicating one of the hazard or the turn, and wherein the light source comprises a second bulb for visually indicating the autonomous mode. 6. The integrated sensor assembly of any of claims 1-5, wherein the integrated sensor assembly further comprises: an electromagnetic interference shield configured to shield the first sensor and from electromagnetic interference. 7. The integrated sensor assembly of any of claims 1-6, wherein the first sensor and the light source are wired to a bus cable of an electrical system, the bus cable extending into the compartment. 8. The integrated sensor assembly of any of claims 1-7, wherein the first sensor is a light detection and ranging (LiDAR) sensor. 9. The integrated sensor assembly of claim 8, wherein the housing further comprises: a second opening for permitting the LiDAR sensor to collect reflected signal from the environment of the autonomous vehicle. 10. The integrated sensor assembly of any of claims 1-9, wherein the integrated sensor assembly is arranged on the side surface of the cabin at a higher elevation than a side-view mirror. 11. A method, comprising: injecting a polymer into a mold of a housing base; cooling the mold to form the housing base, the housing base forming a compartment, and the housing base; arranging a first sensor, the first sensor to be connected to the housing base; arranging a light source in the compartment inward from the first sensor and the second opening; and sealing the second opening of the housing base with a cover plate, the cover plate comprising a third opening for permitting a light from the light source to be emitted to form an integrated sensor assembly. 12. The method of claim 11, the method further comprising: arranging a second sensor in the compartment; and arranging a shield inward from the first sensor and the second sensor, the shield configured to prevent electromagnetic signals from passing through the shield. 13. The method of any of claims 11 or 12, the method further comprising: 

 connecting the housing base to an arm at a fourth opening of the housing base, the fourth opening permitting a bus cable to be extended through the housing base. 14. The method of any of claims 11-13, the method further comprising: arranging the integrated sensor assembly on a side surface of a cabin of an autonomous vehicle proximate to a cabin roof of the autonomous vehicle. 15. The method of any of claims 11-14, the method further comprising: connecting the first sensor and the light source to vehicle computer via a bus cable. 16. A method, comprising: receiving, by an integrated sensor assembly, a first signal from a vehicle computer of an autonomous vehicle; collecting, by the integrated sensor assembly, first sensor information for mapping an environment of the autonomous vehicle at a first time interval and based on the first signal, the integrated sensor assembly arranged on a side surface of a cabin of the autonomous vehicle and proximate to a cabin roof of the autonomous vehicle; and transmitting, by the integrated sensor assembly, the first sensor information to a vehicle computer; receiving, by the integrated sensor assembly, a second signal from the vehicle computer of the autonomous vehicle based on the transmitted sensor information; and emitting, by the integrated sensor assembly, a light indicating a turn based on the second signal at a second time interval. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the first time interval overlaps the second time interval. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the first time interval is distinct from the second time interval. 19. The method of any of claims 16-18, wherein the method further comprises: 

 collecting second sensor information an environment of the autonomous vehicle at a third time interval; transmitting the second sensor information to the vehicle computer; receiving, by an integrated sensor assembly, a third signal from the vehicle computer of the autonomous vehicle based on the transmitted sensor information; and discontinuing emitting the light based on the third signal. 20. The method of any of claims 16-19, wherein the first signal is received, the first sensor information is transmitted, and the second signal is received via a same bus cable.
